title: How to get Document out for SSB Interview
author: Defence Prep Editorial Team
data:
Best guide for what documents to take for NDA,CDS,AFCAT.. SSB
 and eventually not get document out.

content.text: The Ultimate Guide to Getting Documents Out at SSB
Congratulations on receiving your SSB call letter! Reaching this stage is a commendable achievement, and thorough preparation with your paperwork is the first step toward a successful interview.
Ensuring your documents are accurate and complete avoids last-minute panic during verification. Below is the comprehensive list of documents required for your SSB interview.

content: Class 10th Mark Sheet / Matriculation Certificate
Intermediate (10+2) Pass Certificate
For college graduates: Semester-wise independent mark sheets for each semester
Valid Proof of Identity (Original Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Passport, or equivalent government-issued ID)

content.text: Documents for All Graduation / Post-Graduation Entries

content: Graduation Degree / LLB Degree / Provisional Degree / Additional documents required for specific entries clearly showing that you have passed the degree examination and are eligible for the award of the degree.

Final-year candidates: You must complete all examinations (including theory, practicals, projects, viva-voce, backlogs, etc.) related to the degree prior to the 1st day of the respective entry's joining month (Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct as applicable) at the Training Academy. You should be able to produce the degree certificate either before or within 12 weeks of the commencement of the course. In cases where requisite exams are completed but results are awaited, or results are declared but mark sheets/degree certificates are not yet received, candidates must bring a Bonafide Certificate on original letterhead duly signed by the Principal/Head of the Institution (as per Appendix 'B').
No Claim Certificate (Format attached as Appendix 'C').

TGC Entry & SSC (Tech) Men/Women Entry: (1) A Conversion Certificate indicating the conversion formula of grades (CGPA/SGPA/CPI) to percentages on college/university letterhead, signed by the Dean/Principal/Registrar. (2) Declaration Certificate as per Appendix 'D'. (3) If your engineering stream is not listed in the notified streams and you wish to claim equivalence, an Equivalence Certificate issued by the University is mandatory.

NCC Special Entry (Men/Women): (1) NCC 'C' Certificate with a minimum 'B' Grade. (2) Conversion Certificate issued by the University/College for CGPA/Grades systems to ascertain educational eligibility.

JAG Entry (Men/Women): (1) Eligibility for registration with the Bar Council of India/State, along with a copy of authority from the Bar Council stating that the institution is recognized, signed by college/university authorities. (2) Conversion Certificate for CGPA/Grades systems.
UPSC Entries: UPSC Admit Card.
Non-UPSC Entries (Except Service Entries): One copy of the online application duly signed with a self-attested photograph affixed.
NDA Entry: Risk Certificate duly signed by parent/guardian (Format as per Appendix 'E').
10+2 TES Entry: (1) Risk Certificate duly signed by parent/guardian (Appendix 'E'). (2) Declaration Certificate as per Appendix 'D'.
Service candidates will be entertained at the Selection Centre for their SSB interview only if they report on a Movement Order from their respective units.
Latest photocopy of Field Conduct Sheet, duly attested.
Service Identity Card.

content.text: Note: Make sure to carry at least two photocopies of every document listed above alongside your original certificates.
Appendices are standardized forms required under specific conditions during document verification. Ensure you print and complete the relevant ones before heading to the Selection Centre.

content: Appendix A (Bonafide Certificate): Required if your original documents are currently submitted to your college or university.
Appendix B (Result Awaited Certificate): For final-year candidates whose final results or mark sheets are still pending at the time of reporting.
Appendix C (No Claim Certificate): A mandatory declaration form. Carry at least 4 copies.
Appendix D (Declaration Certificate): Required for candidates attending TGC, SSC (Tech), or TES entries. Carry at least 4 copies.
Appendix E (Risk Certificate): Must be signed by your parent or legal guardian (mandatory for NDA and TES entries). Carry at least 4 copies.
Appendix D / Tattoo Certificate: Required if you have permitted body tattoos. Bring 2 copies.
Appendix V (Tribal Tattoo Certificate): Specifically for candidates belonging to tribal communities who have customary body tattoos.
Appendix F (Change of Address): Required only if your current address differs from the one recorded in your application. If not applicable, you do not need copies of this.
Appendix G (Travelling Allowance Form): Required for claiming TA on your first attempt for a specific type of commission. Carry at least 2 copies.
Educational Certificates: Prepare original Class 10th, 12th, and semester mark sheets/degrees, along with at least 4 photocopies of each. It is always safer to carry excess copies.
SSB Call Letter: Carry the official call-up letter along with 4 printed copies.
PIQ Forms: Take at least 4 printed copies of the Personal Information Questionnaire (PIQ) form—2 for submission and 2 for your personal reference.

Passport-Size Photographs: Bring at least 30 to 40 recent passport-sized photographs. While you may not need all of them initially, recommended candidates require multiple sets for paperwork, so carrying extras avoids last-minute hassles.
UPSC Admit Card / Application Form: Bring the original along with 4 photocopies.
Stationery Essentials: Keep a Fevistick (glue stick) and a stapler in your document folder for instant photo attachment and document grouping.

content.text: WARNING: Always read the specific call-up instructions issued recently with your SSB call letter. While the documents listed above are mandatory standard requirements, official guidelines or rules may be updated periodically.

content: Original Documents: 1 set of all educational mark sheets, certificates, degrees, ID proofs, and admit cards/applications
Educational Mark Sheets & Certificates (10th, 12th, Semesters): 4 photocopies each
SSB Call Letter: 4 copies
PIQ Forms: 4 copies (2 for submission, 2 for personal reference)
UPSC Admit Card / Online Application Form: 4 copies
Appendix A (Bonafide - Documents with College): 4 copies (if applicable)
Appendix B (Result Awaited Certificate): 4 copies (if applicable)
Appendix C (No Claim Certificate): 4 copies
Appendix D (Declaration Certificate - TGC/TES): 4 copies
Appendix E (Risk Certificate - NDA/TES): 4 copies
Tattoo Certificate (Appendix D/V): 2 copies (if applicable)
Appendix F (Change of Address): 2 copies (only if address changed)
Appendix G (Travelling Allowance Form): 2 copies (if claiming TA)
Passport-Size Photographs: 30 to 40 photos
General Photocopies Rule: At least 2 to 4 photocopies for every additional document or certificate
